customer_goal: Keep the vectorized custom Gaussian renderer useful at larger simultaneous camera batches without weakening output, fidelity, Isaac integration, or CUDA correctness gates.

verdict.summary: Replacing the two dense per-tile range arrays with int32 endpoints saves 47,185,920 bytes at Home B40 and changes the exact 240-view B40 case from a physical-memory OOM into a full acceptance pass. Home and Garage B8 throughput is unchanged within run-to-run noise, strict 72-view custom-versus-gsplat fidelity passes, the finite Isaac/CUDA ladder passes, and Compute Sanitizer reports zero errors.
verdict.performance_interpretation: This is a capacity optimization, not a new rendering speedup. Home B40 sustains 39.29 GPU images/s, essentially the same throughput plateau as B8. The previously established B8 speedup versus pinned gsplat remains valid; this change preserves it while allowing a larger exact batch.
verdict.customer_interpretation: The result is useful for high-throughput vectorized Gaussian observations that fit the reported L4 protocol. It does not establish 24 Hz batch latency, free memory for a co-resident RTX workload, an L40 result, or a replacement for RTX rendering.

source.retained_renderer_change: Dense tile_starts and tile_ends workspace arrays, the deterministic grouping path, the emitted-prefix range marker, and the compositor range inputs now use int32 endpoints.
source.retained_evidence_change: Isaac trajectory results now report backend workspace bytes by tensor.
source.validation_fix: The multi-scene CUDA smoke now checks the documented inactive-camera background policy instead of incorrectly requiring stale output from the previous full render.

protocol.coverage: Every source view appears exactly once in each reported case.
protocol.custom_path: Headless Isaac Sim MinimalRendering, USD/Fabric CUDA camera ingestion, compact projection cache, emitted-prefix global radix ordering, output-contract validation, and artifact bookkeeping.
protocol.fidelity_reference: Pinned gsplat contract-exact replay of the persisted Garage B8 trajectory using the same deterministic screen-space EWA equation family.
protocol.warmup: Shader, JIT, extension, adaptive-capacity, and renderer warmup occur outside acceptance timing.
output_requirements: foreground and background semantic policy valid
zero visible or intersection capacity overflow

baseline_b40.failure_stage: Mandatory depth output-contract validation after the renderer completed.
baseline_b40.failure: CUDA OOM requesting 108.00 MiB with 103.38 MiB free; the process used 21.89 GiB of the 22.03 GiB L4 and PyTorch had allocated 20.95 GiB.
baseline_b40.reproduction: The unchanged baseline was reproduced on the replacement L4 before testing candidate changes.

hypotheses.name: mode-aware CUB sort scratch sizing
hypotheses.measured_effect: zero bytes saved in the failing nondeterministic B40 path
hypotheses.action: fully reverted before the retained commit

hypotheses.name: int32 dense tile-range endpoints
hypotheses.reason: The native implementation already rejects more than INT_MAX intersections and more than INT_MAX tile segments, so all inclusive/exclusive endpoints are exactly representable in signed int32.
hypotheses.effect: Halves the footprint of tile_starts and tile_ends without changing the rendering equation or ordering policy.

savings.note: The 16,936-byte difference beyond the fixed range-array saving comes from a 471-intersection adaptive-capacity difference between independent runs.

profiling.case: Home B8 exact moving trajectory at 384x384
profiling.scope: Full-process trace with two warmup batches and 30 measured batches. Adaptive retry work remains in the kernel-time numerator; 256 useful views are used for the per-view diagnostic.
profiling.diagnosis: The memory change does not regress the profiled workload. Global radix sorting remains the largest kernel stage and is the next performance target.

finite_gpu_ladder.deterministic_cuda_smoke: pass, including bitwise repeatability, capacity invariance, and equal-depth Gaussian-ID ordering
finite_gpu_ladder.isaac_headless_smoke: pass with success-marker and fatal-log checks
finite_gpu_ladder.kit_extension_smoke: pass with CustomCudaBackend on cuda:0 and Fabric Scene Delegate enabled
finite_gpu_ladder.fabric_camera_smoke: pass with CUDA output and zero-copy Torch handoff
finite_gpu_ladder.multi_scene_cuda_smoke: pass after correcting its stale inactive-output expectation
finite_gpu_ladder.projection_cache_cuda_smoke: pass with compact projection, coherent cache events, CUDA outputs, and zero overflow

validation_bug_found.description: The production CUDA backend intentionally resets inactive camera slots to defined background values, but multi_scene_cuda_smoke.py still required those slots to preserve the previous render. The corrected test now asserts RGB=0, depth=+inf, alpha=0, and semantic_id=-1.

remaining_bottlenecks.next_target: Reduce or stream the emitted-intersection global-sort working set. The paired key/value buffers and CUB scratch consume 95.82% of the B40 workspace, so further tile-range micro-optimizations cannot create material RTX co-residency headroom.
remaining_bottlenecks.profiling_basis: Sorting is 38.39% of Home B8 kernel time, making it both the largest remaining runtime stage and the dominant memory family.

limitations: The successful B40 sample used 22,485 MiB of driver-reported process memory on a 23,034 MiB L4. That is not enough evidence to promise safe co-residency with a substantial Isaac RTX workload.
All reported batches miss the 41.67 ms per-batch latency target; throughput per image and synchronous observation latency are different metrics.
The B40 custom case has no matched gsplat B40 throughput because the comparison target is physical capacity recovery, not a new B40 speedup claim.
No L40 result is included. The result must be rerun on the intended L40 before making L40-specific customer claims.
The strict fidelity gate compares the same screen-space EWA equation family. OVRTX perspective fidelity and RTX compositing remain separate questions.
Nsight Systems timing is diagnostic and includes adaptive retry work. No Nsight Compute hardware-counter claim is made.

evidence.availability: Raw artifacts are intentionally ignored by Git; checksums bind this committed record to the preserved local evidence.
